column6:
There was no aptitude, reasoning, or verbal ability test for the first round. Instead, the company gave a project assignment to complete within a deadline.
 The company asked us to complete a project based on the document they provided. The task was to build a multi-agent tourism system where the user could enter a place and receive information such as weather updates and tourist attractions. The project required integrating external APIs for weather, geocoding, and places data. I developed the complete project as per the requirements and submitted it within the given deadline.
column9:
In the second round, I had a virtual live coding session based on the project I completed in the first round. The interviewer first asked me to run the project, provide a sample prompt, and show the output. After that, I explained the complete workflow of the project and the approach I followed while building it.
 
 Next, the interviewer gave me an additional task to extend the existing project by adding a “budget plan for the places” feature. I wrote the code for this requirement during the session and explained each step as I implemented it.
 
 Towards the end of the interview, the interviewer asked me to describe all the other files present in the project and explain their purpose without referring to the project directory. The round mainly focused on understanding, clarity of explanation, coding ability, and how well I knew my own project.
column10: The technical questions in the session were mainly focused on the project I implemented for the first round.
column11: The interviewer gave me a task to extend my existing project during the live coding session.
column13: The project was based on building a multi-agent tourism.
